subject: Mathematics
title: Cubes and Prisms
description: A 60-second lesson on Cubes and Prisms.

hook.text: A cube has six identical square faces, twelve edges, and eight corners. It's geometry's building block.

buildup.text: A prism is any shape with two identical parallel faces connected by rectangles.

discovery.text: A prism's volume is simply the base area times its height. Straightforward and universal.

twist.text: Euler discovered a rule: for any convex polyhedron, vertices minus edges plus faces equals 2.

climax.text: Buildings, boxes, and crystals are all prisms. They stack, pack, and fill space efficiently.

punchline.text: The humble cube packs space better than almost any shape.

quiz.question: How do you calculate the volume of any prism?
